title: Bound memory growth to avoid the OOM killer
description: Cap buffers and in-flight work and stream large inputs so the process cannot grow until the kernel kills it.
body: Use when processing inputs of unknown size or accumulating state. Do not read an entire large file/response into memory or grow an unbounded cache, because under load resident memory climbs until the kernel OOM-killer terminates the process (often the largest, not the guilty one). Actions: stream large inputs in fixed chunks, bound caches and queues with eviction, and size against the worst-case input and the host/cgroup memory limit. Validation signals include flat RSS under large inputs and no OOM kills in a sustained load test. Risk boundary: unbounded buffering is a crash-under-load hazard; if a fix changes what is dropped or evicted, confirm it cannot lose committed data and escalate if it does.
